rezs.name: Central-West Orana REZ
rezs.infrastructure_cost_note: Massive blowout from original estimates. Quarterly payments to ACEREZ over 2025-29 total $1.51B. Financial close 4 Apr 2025.
rezs.community_benefit_detail: Community benefit & employment program funded via access fees
issues: AEMO reports transmission costs 100% higher than 2024 estimates
Community complaints about lack of consultation

rezs.name: New England REZ
rezs.access_fee_note: Draft Access Scheme Declaration expected end Jun 2026. Formal declaration by Minister end Sep 2026. Expected to follow CWO model.
rezs.infrastructure_cost_note: Stage 1 ~$2.1B (220 km dual 500 kV Bayswater to NE REZ + 100 km 500 kV between hubs). Stage 2 ~$3B additional. Costs have actually fallen ~$3B from earlier estimates.

rezs.name: Hunter-Central Coast REZ
rezs.network_operator_note: Only NSW REZ on a distribution network (not Transgrid)
rezs.capacity_detail: 1 GW network capacity enabling ~1.8 GW generation/storage
rezs.access_fee_note: Access scheme design in progress. Expected to follow NSW REZ model.
rezs.infrastructure_cost_note: 2 new substations, major upgrades to 2 existing, ~85 km powerline upgrades. Ausgrid signed project deed Dec 2025.

rezs.name: South-West REZ
rezs.access_fee_note: Access scheme formally declared. Follows CWO model (annual, CPI-indexed, community benefit). Specific $/MW not disclosed.
rezs.infrastructure_cost_note: Leverages Project EnergyConnect and VNI West. $183M road infrastructure investment (incl $65M for SW REZ). Expected to attract $17.8B private investment.
rezs.community_benefit_detail: Community and Employment Benefit Program funded via access fees

sources.title: EnergyCo SW REZ Access Scheme declared

rezs.name: Illawarra REZ
rezs.network_operator_note: NSW's first urban REZ — leveraging distribution network
rezs.access_fee_note: Very early stage. No access scheme or fees defined.

rezs.name: Victorian REZ Framework (VicGrid)
rezs.access_model_note: Victorian Access Regime (VAR) — regulatory, not competitive tender. Came into effect mid-March 2026.
rezs.access_fee_note: Fees recover: admin costs, Community Energy Funds, Traditional Owners Fund. Specific $/MW TBD — to be detailed in VicGrid consultation paper.
rezs.infrastructure_cost_note: Almost doubled from initial $4.3B draft. Increases from materials, labour, supply chain constraints.

timeline.completion_target: Rolling — REZ orders delayed to mid-2026

rezs.community_benefit_detail: Community Energy Funds and Traditional Owners benefits mandated. Community engagement is a condition of grid access.
rezs.access_caps_detail: Caps on solar and wind capacity within each REZ to avoid curtailment
rezs.key_difference: Regulatory regime (not competitive tender). VicGrid has both transmission planning AND connection responsibilities (unique centralised role).

rezs.name: Queensland SuperGrid
rezs.access_model_note: Generator-pays model. No competitive tender for access rights. Market-led approach through Powerlink.
rezs.access_fee_note: Connection fees via standard Powerlink negotiation. Government underwrites residual costs not recovered from generators.
rezs.infrastructure_cost_note: $62B total SuperGrid investment over 10-15 years (transmission ~11%). Landholder Payment Framework: ~$300k/km.
rezs.community_benefit_detail: Landholder payments ~$300,000 per km for hosting new transmission. $6M for Central QLD Strategic REZ Readiness. $20M Regional Economic Futures Fund.
rezs.key_difference: No competitive auction for access rights. Market-led, generator-pays through Powerlink. Government underwrites residual.
issues: QLD rejected Nelson Review / ESEM successor to CIS
QLD rejected AEMO last-resort gas market powers

rezs.name: South Australia (No Formal REZ Scheme)
rezs.access_model_note: No formal REZ access fee regime. Developers connect through standard NEM processes via ElectraNet.
rezs.access_fee_note: No state-run REZ access scheme, no access fees, no competitive tender.
rezs.key_difference: The outlier. No state-run REZ access scheme. Pursuing path under Hydrogen and Renewable Energy Act. AEMO identifies 9 zones but SA not implementing formal access.

access_models.sa: No formal REZ scheme — standard NEM connection

cost_blowouts.nsw_ne: $5B+ (but costs actually fell $3B from earlier estimates)

industry_concerns: Transmission cost escalation (materials, labour, supply chain)
Delays in scheme design pushing out investment decisions
Community opposition and land access disputes
Uncertainty about final access fee quantum deterring proponents
AEMO reports transmission costs sometimes 100% higher than 2024 estimates

cis_successor.name: Electricity Services Entry Mechanism (ESEM)
cis_successor.qld_position: Rejected Nelson Review core recommendations and AEMO last-resort gas powers
